essence: Builds and maintains the data pipelines, systems, and infrastructure that collect, process, and deliver CNC machine data at scale.

identity.title: CNC Data Engineer
identity.summary: Designs and manages industrial data pipelines that capture CNC machine data, transform it into usable formats, and support analytics, optimisation, and Industry 4.0 initiatives.

salary.notes: Engineers in aerospace, defence, or fully digitalised smart‑factory environments can exceed £120k-£160k+.

qualifications: Experience with CNC data systems, IIoT, MES, or SCADA
Knowledge of data pipelines, ETL/ELT, and industrial communication protocols
Skills in databases, APIs, and real‑time data processing
Understanding of CNC machine behaviour, performance metrics, and manufacturing workflows

lifestyle.description: CNC data engineers work in advanced manufacturing, aerospace, automotive, robotics, and Industry 4.0 environments. They build the data infrastructure that powers analytics, automation, and optimisation. The role suits individuals who enjoy data systems, industrial technology, and enabling digital transformation.

habits: Builds and maintains CNC data pipelines
Connects machines to IIoT, MES, and SCADA platforms
Processes real‑time and historical machining data
Ensures data quality, reliability, and accessibility
Collaborates with analysts, engineers, and automation teams
